senses.gloss: form, mould, last (for casting shapes by it)

etymology.text: From Classical Syriac ܩܠܒܝܕ (/⁠*qālobīḏ⁠/, “last”), from Ancient Greek καλοπόδιον (kalopódion, “last”), diminutive of καλόπους (kalópous, “last”), compound of κᾶλον (kâlon, “wood”) and πούς (poús, “foot”).

body.text: , (S, MA, O, Msb, K, KL,) and ↓

a mould into which metals are poured:
is an arabicized word, as is shown by its form, which is not that of an Arabic word; though Esh-Shiháb, in his Expos. of the Shifè, denies this: its original is [the Pers. word]

body.text: He has returned in reply the model,
pattern, of speech; and has hit the joint so as to sever the limb;
agreeably with an explanation in art.
and has put the tar upon the places of the scabs;

(to which I cannot assign in this case any apposite meaning) instead of

, (O, L, TA,) with fet-h and with kesr to the
[q. v.]: in this sense likewise said to be an arabicized word: and

,] occurring in a trad., in which it is said that the women of the Children of Israel used to wear the wooden sandals thus called: (L, TA:) it is related in a trad. of Ibn-Mes'ood that the woman used to wear a pair of the kind of sandals thus called in order thereby to elevate herself (O, L, TA) when the men and the women of that people used to pray together. (O.)
(S, O, Msb, K:) so in the dial. of Belhárith Ibn-Kaab: (El-Umawee, TA:) [app. an epithet in which the quality of a subst. is predominant; for
an unripe date when it has become wholly altered
she-goat, of a colour different from that of her mother:
